skill.name: software-architecture
skill.description: Guides software design, code structure, and architecture reviews using clean architecture and domain-driven design principles.
skill.summary: Apply clean architecture, domain-driven design, and code quality guidance to software development work.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance patterns, but review found only markdown examples and architecture terminology. No executable code, prompt injection, network access, filesystem access, or credential handling was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Spans Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: Lines 27, 43, 44, 64, 65, and 66 contain inline markdown examples such as library names, class names, and file names. They are documentation text, not Ruby backtick execution or shell command instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is inside markdown inline code spans and names non-executable examples. No command invocation syntax or instruction to execute those strings appears in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Architecture Terms Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Lines 3, 8, 35, and 38 discuss software architecture, clean architecture, and domain-driven design. No weak cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, cipher configuration, or cryptography guidance is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The terms are ordinary architecture prose. I did not find evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: General Design Guidance Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Lines 15, 53, and 73 describe reusable modules, anti-patterns, and nesting limits. No system enumeration, host inspection, environment probing, or discovery command is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are high-level coding recommendations. They do not request or describe commands that gather system information.

content.value_statement: Software projects often lose clarity as features, dependencies, and teams grow. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code guide architecture, naming, boundaries, and maintainable implementation choices.
actual_capabilities: Applies clean architecture and domain-driven design principles to coding tasks.
Encourages early returns, focused functions, and smaller files for readable code.
Recommends library-first decisions before custom helper or utility code.
Identifies generic naming and unclear module boundaries as architecture risks.
Guides separation between business logic, UI components, controllers, and database access.
limitations: Does not include executable tools, scripts, or automated code analysis.
Does not replace project-specific architecture decision records or team standards.
Provides general guidance and may need adaptation for strict framework conventions.
Does not validate third-party library security, licensing, or maintenance status.

use_cases.title: Review a Feature Design
use_cases.description: Assess module boundaries, domain language, and dependency choices before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Refactor Growing Code
use_cases.description: Find large files, mixed responsibilities, and generic helpers that should move into clearer modules.

use_cases.title: Guide Implementation Choices
use_cases.description: Choose between existing libraries, SaaS services, third-party APIs, and justified custom code.

prompt_templates.title: Check Basic Structure
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a planned feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this feature plan for clean architecture basics. Identify unclear responsibilities, missing boundaries, and simple improvements before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Naming and Modules
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate refactor of existing code organization.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this module layout and naming. Suggest domain-specific names, clearer boundaries, and files that should be split or merged.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Build Versus Buy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced decision about dependencies and custom code.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate whether this capability should use an existing library, SaaS service, third-party API, or custom code. Include maintenance and security tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Bounded Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced architecture design for a larger domain.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design bounded contexts for this domain. Define entities, use cases, infrastructure boundaries, and where business logic should live.

output_examples.input: Review a checkout feature that mixes payment logic, UI state, and database writes in one component.
output_examples.output: The review separates payment rules into a domain service, moves persistence behind an infrastructure boundary, and keeps UI code focused on interaction state.

output_examples.input: Decide whether to write custom retry logic for a background job processor.
output_examples.output: The response recommends evaluating established retry libraries first, then lists conditions that could justify custom code, such as strict domain behavior or unusual performance needs.

output_examples.input: Improve a project with files named helpers, utils, and shared.
output_examples.output: The response proposes domain-based module names, removes unrelated functions from shared files, and assigns each module a single clear purpose.

best_practices: Share domain context, current file structure, and known framework constraints before asking for architecture advice.
Ask for tradeoffs when choosing between libraries, services, APIs, and custom implementation.
Validate recommendations against team standards, security requirements, and operational constraints.
anti_patterns: Using the skill as a substitute for concrete product requirements or system constraints.
Applying clean architecture mechanically to small code paths that do not need extra layers.
Accepting dependency recommendations without checking license, maintenance, and security status.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps structure software around clear boundaries, domain concepts, readable code, and maintainable architecture choices.

faq.question: Does it run code or inspect my system?
faq.answer: No. The skill is markdown guidance only and does not include scripts, commands, or automated scanners.

faq.question: Can it review existing code?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide relevant files or summaries, and the assistant can identify architecture and code quality issues.

faq.question: Does it require clean architecture for every project?
faq.answer: No. It provides principles that should be adapted to project size, complexity, and team conventions.

faq.question: Can it choose third-party libraries?
faq.answer: It can guide library-first evaluation, but users should verify security, licensing, maintenance, and compatibility.

faq.question: Is it suitable for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
